prompt: shrink this codebase — it's gotten really bloated
expected_output: Four subagents run in parallel (dead code hunter, ghost file hunter, code consolidator, verbosity reducer), baseline LOC is measured, tests are run before any changes, findings are applied in safe batches, and a final report shows lines removed per category with before/after test counts.
expectations: Skill triggers on 'shrink this codebase' phrasing
Establishes baseline LOC before any changes
Runs tests before making changes
Spawns at least 4 parallel subagents
Writes findings JSON files to /tmp/shrinkray-output/
Writes STAGED_CHANGES.md before applying any edits
Runs tests after applying changes
Reverts changes that cause test failures
Never runs git commit
Prints final report with LOC before and after

prompt: remove all the dead code from this repo and make it smaller
expected_output: Skill triggers (dead code removal + make smaller = shrinkray scope). Four subagents deployed. Dead Code Hunter finds unused functions/imports/variables, Ghost File Hunter finds unreferenced files, both report with confidence levels. Changes applied and tests verified.
expectations: Skill triggers on 'remove dead code' combined with size reduction intent
Dead Code Hunter subagent specifically searches for unreachable code
Ghost File Hunter searches for unreferenced files
Each change is logged with category and estimated LOC saved
Tests pass after all changes
Final report breaks down lines removed by category

expected_output: Explicit skill invocation. Full pipeline executes: Phase 0 setup, 4 parallel subagents, triage, apply, verify, convergence loop.
expectations: Skill triggers on explicit 'run shrinkray' command
Detects test runner automatically
Measures baseline LOC
Spawns all four subagents simultaneously
Applies changes with per-change syntax checks
Runs full test suite after changes
Stops when converged or 5 iterations elapsed

prompt: can you review this code and find bugs?
expected_output: Skill does NOT trigger. Bug finding without size reduction is code-smellz territory or a simple review. Shrinkray should be skipped.
expectations: Skill does NOT trigger on pure bug-finding requests
Claude answers the review request directly or suggests code-smellz instead

prompt: minify my JavaScript bundle for production
expected_output: Skill does NOT trigger. JS bundle minification (webpack/esbuild) is a build tool concern, not a source code restructuring task.
expectations: Skill does NOT trigger on JS/CSS bundle minification requests
Claude suggests using the appropriate build tool instead

prompt: Trim one verbose helper function in helpers.py — it's the only obvious bloat in the file.
expected_output: Takes the lite tier: reviews that one function directly for verbosity, skipping the four-agent crew.
expectations: Takes the lite tier — does not spawn all four agents to trim a single function
Shortens the helper without changing behavior
